title: Supercharging Liferay DXP Q1 2026 with AI: A Deep Dive into MCP Integration using Antigravity
author: Pyronite Tech
label: NEW / Liferay DXP Q1 2026
excerpt: At Pyronite Tech, we are constantly exploring ways to elevate enterprise architecture. With the release of Liferay DXP Q1 2026, the platform has introduced a game-changing capability...
metaDescription: A definitive guide on supercharging Liferay DXP Q1 2026 with AI. Discover how to configure the Model Context Protocol (MCP) securely with Antigravity.
metaKeywords: Liferay DXP Q1 2026, Liferay MCP, Model Context Protocol, Liferay AI Integration, Antigravity, Headless Liferay, Pyronite Tech

content.html: At Pyronite Tech, we are constantly exploring ways to elevate enterprise architecture. With the release of Liferay DXP Q1 2026, the platform has introduced a game-changing capability: native support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP). By connecting Liferay to an AI assistant like Antigravity via MCP, we can transition Liferay from a traditional portal into a dynamic, AI-driven platform.

This integration allows the AI to securely read your portal's context, fetch OpenAPIs, and execute headless operations directly from your prompt interface. However, because this feature is currently in beta, configuring it requires some precision.
Here is the definitive, step-by-step guide on how we successfully configured Antigravity with Liferay DXP Q1 2026.

content.text: Step 1: Enable the Beta Feature Flag in Liferay

content.html: Out of the box, the MCP endpoint in Liferay is hidden behind a feature flag. If you skip this step, the server simply will not respond to connection requests.

items: Navigate to your Liferay DXP configuration environment or Control Panel.
Restart your Liferay bundle to ensure the endpoint (/o/mcp) is exposed.

content.text: Step 2: Create a Dedicated Service Account & Generate Credentials

content.html: Security is paramount when giving an AI agent access to your enterprise platform. You should never use a full Administrator account for this integration.

items: Create the User: Set up a dedicated user (e.g., mcp-ai@pyronite.in) in Liferay.
Assign Granular Roles: Create a custom role that only has View or Read permissions for the specific APIs (like Headless Delivery or User Management) the AI needs to interact with.
Generate the Basic Auth Token: Liferay requires Basic Authentication via a Base64 encoded string of your username:password.

content.text: Step 3: Configure Antigravity to Communicate with Liferay

content.html: Antigravity requires a strictly formatted JSON configuration file to establish the Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection with Liferay.
Add the following JSON block. We utilize the npx mcp-remote command to reliably handle the SSE connection bridge, which solves common IDE validation errors:

content.caption: Fig 1. MCP JSON Config in Antigravity

items: You must have Node.js installed on your machine for the npx command to execute successfully.

The --header "Accept: text/event-stream" argument is mandatory. Without it, Liferay will reject the connection with a 400 Bad Request and an "text/event-stream required" error, causing Antigravity to hang in an infinite loading loop.

content.text: Step 4: Verify and Start Using AI as a Platform

content.html: Once the JSON is saved, completely restart Antigravity (ensure you kill any lingering background node.exe processes via Task Manager to clear the cache).
When you reopen Antigravity, navigate to your MCP Servers panel. You should see liferay listed with 3/3 tools enabled. These built-in tools instantly empower your AI workflow:

items: get-openapis: The AI can now discover all available Liferay OpenAPIs dynamically.
get-openapi: Fetches the specific YAML structure for a chosen API, allowing the AI to understand exactly what parameters are required.
call-http-endpoint: The AI can execute actual read/write operations (based on the permissions of your service account) directly against your Liferay instance.

content.caption: Fig 2. Completed MCP Liferay configuration in Antigravity

content.html: Before prompting the AI, here is our Liferay environment showing the current list of sites available:

content.alt: List of sites in Liferay before prompt
content.caption: Fig 3. List of sites in Liferay before any prompt

content.html: Instead of spending hours reading through API documentation or writing boilerplate REST calls, our developers can now simply open the Antigravity chat and ask:

content.alt: Prompting to create a site in Liferay
content.caption: Fig 4. Prompting to create a site in Liferay

content.html: The AI automatically invokes get-openapis to find the correct endpoint, understands the schema via get-openapi, and executes the fetch using call-http-endpoint.

content.caption: Fig 5. Site successfully created in Liferay

content.html: By integrating MCP with Liferay DXP, we are no longer just building portals; we are creating intelligent, self-documenting, and context-aware enterprise platforms.
